title: Business Intent Model v0.1
description: The Business Intent Model (BIM) captures what the business wants, in business language. It is human-authorable, may be partial, and is allowed to contain ambiguity that the AI translation pipeline resolves into the Application Intent Model (AIM). See ADR-011 for the full BIM vs AIM contract. This is a v0.1 draft schema; expect additive minor bumps as the reference scenarios grow.

$comment.title: Authoring comment
$comment.description: Optional free-text authoring comment. Ignored by the platform; useful for humans reading the raw JSON.

bimVersion.title: BIM document version
bimVersion.description: Semantic version of this BIM document. Independent of the BIM schema version. Bumped when the business intent changes.

changeHistory.title: Change history
changeHistory.description: Ordered log of meaningful edits to this BIM, with the reason for each change. Most recent last is recommended but not enforced.

personas.title: Personas
personas.description: Named business actors involved with the capability. Business names, not canonical role IDs.

businessObjects.title: Business objects
businessObjects.description: The things the business talks about (customer, case, document...). Attributes are described in business language; typing is deferred to the AIM.

businessRules.title: Business rules
businessRules.description: Business policies stated in prose, optionally illustrated with examples. Formalization into rule expressions happens in the AIM.

actions.title: Actions
actions.description: Verbs the personas perform against the capability. These become semantic operations in the AIM.

nonFunctional.title: Non-functional expectations
nonFunctional.description: Soft expectations expressed in business language ('should be fast', 'must be secure'). Resolved to concrete budgets/policies in the AIM.

openQuestions.title: Open questions
openQuestions.description: Unresolved questions the clarification agent (or a human) needs to answer before the BIM can be translated cleanly.

attachments.title: Attachments
attachments.description: References to external artifacts (transcripts, screenshots, sample forms) that provide additional context.

semverString.description: Semantic version string (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH), optionally with a pre-release suffix.

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description: Descriptive information about this BIM document.

id.title: BIM identifier
id.description: Stable identifier for this BIM. Must start with 'bim.' followed by a lowercase kebab-case slug.

name.title: Capability name
name.description: Human-friendly name of the capability, in business language.

shortDescription.title: Short description
shortDescription.description: One or two sentences describing what this capability does for the business.

industry.title: Industry
industry.description: Target industry or vertical for this capability.

primaryOutcome.title: Primary outcome
primaryOutcome.description: The single most important business outcome delivered when the capability runs successfully.

businessOwner.title: Business owner
businessOwner.description: The role or person accountable for this capability from a business perspective.

authoredBy.title: Authored by
authoredBy.description: Who currently owns editing this BIM document.

language.title: Authoring language
language.description: BCP-47-style language tag for the prose in this BIM (e.g., 'en', 'en-US', 'fr').

status.title: Status
status.description: Editorial status of this BIM document.

summary.description: What changed, in one or two sentences.

rationale.description: Why the change was made. Recommended for any non-cosmetic edit.

capability.title: Capability
capability.description: The core capability the BIM describes.

statement.description: One-paragraph statement of what the capability does, in the customer's own words.

included.description: What is in scope for this version of the capability.

excluded.description: What is explicitly out of scope. Helpful for the AI to avoid over-reaching.

successMetrics.description: How the business will know the capability is working. Stated in business terms; formal SLIs come later.

name.description: Business name of the persona (e.g., 'Bank Officer'). Canonicalization to role IDs happens in the AIM.
The attribute described in business language. Formal typing is deferred to the AIM.

required.description: Whether the business considers this attribute mandatory. Ambiguity is allowed and can be refined during clarification.

allowedValues.description: Enumerated business-language values, when the business already knows them.

note.description: Free-form note or example clarifying the attribute.

workflow.title: Workflow
workflow.description: The end-to-end lifecycle of the capability described as ordered stages in prose. The AIM turns this into a formal state machine.

description.description: One-paragraph description of how the workflow behaves overall.

notes.description: Cross-cutting notes about the workflow (retention, reopen policy, etc.).

id.description: Stable identifier so the rule can be referenced from other documents. Must start with 'rule.' followed by kebab-case.

statement.description: The rule in one or two sentences, in business language.

examples.description: Concrete illustrations that help both the AI and human reviewers understand the intent.

verb.description: The action as a business-language verb phrase (e.g., 'Submit application').

performedBy.description: Persona names that perform this action. Must match names in the 'personas' list.

documents.title: Documents
documents.description: The set of document types the capability must accept, described in business terms.

sizeLimitNote.description: Approximate size expectations in business terms. Concrete byte limits come from the AIM/CAM.

expectation.description: What the business expects, in prose. Resolved to concrete budgets/policies in the AIM.

id.description: Stable identifier for the question. Must start with 'q.' followed by kebab-case.

askedOf.description: Who is expected to answer (role or person).

reference.description: URI, workspace-relative path, or content hash pointing at the attachment.
